asos-next-py-2.4.name: ASOS-Next Data Acquisition
asos-next-py-2.4.description: Current-generation ASOS acquisition stack. Sensor values are ingested as IEEE-754 floats, QC'd, and packed into METAR/SPECI without narrow integer truncation in the wind pathway.

wind_speed.notes: Negative speeds rejected by QC before archival.

awos-c-cpp-1.8.name: AWOS-C Site Controller
awos-c-cpp-1.8.description: FAA AWOS-C controller firmware. Wind is stored as a 16-bit unsigned integer in 0.1 m/s counts.

wind_speed.notes: Saturates at max count; does not wrap to negative.

asos-classic-c-5.1.name: ASOS Classic Acquisition
asos-classic-c-5.1.description: Widely deployed mid-life ASOS build. Wind speed uses a signed 16-bit integer in knots.

wind_speed.notes: QC clamps negatives to missing; practical range 0–250 kt.

asos-ltu-f77-3.2.name: ASOS Legacy Telemetry Unit (LTU)
asos-ltu-f77-3.2.description: Legacy LTU telemetry still operating at a minority of ASOS sites. The acquisition loop and METAR element packer were written in FORTRAN 77 and have not been migrated to the ASOS-Next stack.
asos-ltu-f77-3.2.source_notes: Primary wind packing routine: WNDPAK (WIND PACK) in wndpak.f. Wind speed is read from the anemometer pulse counter, converted to integer mph, and stored in a single-byte INTEGER*1 field before message assembly.

wind_speed.notes: Signed 8-bit two's-complement field. Design assumption in the original LTU ICD was that reported wind speed would never exceed 127 mph. Values of 128 mph and above overflow and wrap (e.g. 129 → -127). There is no saturating clamp in WNDPAK.

known_limitations: Wind speed packed as signed INTEGER*1 (max +127 before overflow).
No runtime check for anemometer readings above 127 mph.
Maintenance bulletins recommend ASOS-Next cutover; several plains sites remain pending.
